title: Detached containers leak network namespaces after deletion
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

/kind bug

**Description**

There are lingering network namespaces after a cycle of heavy podman use, and subsequently removing all containers (not necessarily all at once). As such, `podman rm` does not seem to reap network namespaces under all circumstances. It seems to be linked to detached containers.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -d fedora sleep 5`

2. `podman rm -l`

**Describe the results you received:**

An instance `nsfs` present in `mount` after all Podman containers removed

**Describe the results you expected:**

No `nsfs` present after all Podman containers removed

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Only happens on detached containers

comments.body: @mheon Isn't this why we were looking at adding a post call to conmon to call a cleanup function?
@rhatdan `podman rm` should be removing them regardless - but it may well have something to do with that.
Does podman rm umount them if they are mounted?
It calls `cleanup()` which should unmount and remove the namespaces.

Possibly related, in [the CI logs from another PR][1]:

```
/tmp/test_runner.output =====
time="2018-05-31T20:22:47Z" level=error msg="could not create new watcher too many open files" 
...a number of other "too many open files" errors...
time="2018-05-31T20:23:27Z" level=error msg="could not create new watcher too many open files" 
time="2018-05-31T20:23:37Z" level=error msg="Failed to tear down network namespace for container 41ddc9bfa634fce380b05615bddbc3adb7d765591334c7635290c13b9be9d7d2: cni config uninitialized" 
time="2018-05-31T20:23:47Z" level=error msg="Failed to tear down network namespace for container 8aee1d2ba0143b3c8aaa48b0545a78991a2b055499f42540ca162e148215ead9: cni config uninitialized" 
time="2018-05-31T20:23:47Z" level=error msg="Failed to tear down network namespace for container f32b49152a8caff2972a4315dbe544703f2a70d54dc715cc396dfc1b0109e3c2: cni config uninitialized" 
```

So maybe a CNI config issue?

[1]: https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/a492f1d179071a6729406aa1d19a3d4eff0081e2.2.1527797417223330604/output.log
@giuseppe We need to get conmon calling cleanup code.
RIght but we need to plumb this into podman so that it uses it to cleanup on detatched containers.

Perhaps have a call like:
```
podman containers cleanup CID?
```
I've confirmed that even commands that should run `cleanup()` are not removing the network namespace (in this case, `podman stop` on a detached container).
Correction, `stop()` just calls `cleanupStorage()`. That needs to be fixed...
Confirmed that, even with a full cleanup, the network ns is still being left open. It looks like anything in a new process won't remove the network ns on `cleanup()`

Alright, I think I've located the offending code.

https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/master/vendor/github.com/containernetworking/plugins/pkg/ns/ns_linux.go#L127-L135

`ns.mounted` is only ever set when creating a new namespace, not opening an existing one. As such, when we open the namespace in a new process to clean it up, it will not execute this cleanup code.
The simplest way to deal with this is probably to fire the unmount and remove unconditionally and ignore EEXIST and the like
